goal: Poster shares should fall back to the default-deck FACE before a card back when the active deck is missing a card (BUG-020 / HANDOFF §2D)
done: resolvePosterCardImageSources (share/poster.js) now inserts toDefaultDeckFaceUrl(uprightUrl/reversedUrl/primary) into the fallbackChain just before backUrl. Previously only the daily poster had the cross-deck face fallback; routing it through the shared resolver extends it to the question / full / celtic posters too. No-op on the default deck (toDefaultDeckFaceUrl returns null when active===moonmallow). Does not change the resolvePosterCardImageSources call order or the primary selection (rule #4 preserved); purely additive to the degradation chain.
root_cause: resolvePosterCardImageSources' fallbackChain went straight to backUrl, so a card missing/blocked in the active deck shared a generic card BACK instead of real art — gutting the visual-proof share (hard rule #8). Only the daily poster carried the cross-deck swap.
fix: Insert the default-deck (moonmallow) face of the same card (identical nn-slug-orientation.webp filename) before backUrl in the fallback chain, for all poster modes.
verified: Browser (chrome-devtools): with boba active + a missing front, fallbackChain = [moonmallow face, boba back, moonmallow back] (face before back). With moonmallow active, chain is backs-only (clean no-op, no regression). asset-resolver untouched.
notes: Improves the missing-art case (e.g. veila three/four-of-cups). A CORS-canvas-tainting broken poster (suspected iOS Safari) is a separate cause still pending a user screenshot.
